description: Explore protocol and service architecture for the finalized Fabric implementation baseline: shared resolved bundle view, get_rules/plan_context boundaries, MCP payload evolution, and backward-compatible API shape.
findings: Architecture baseline is already centered on a shared resolver: loadGetRulesContext() caches meta/L0/human-lock data and resolveRulesForPath() constructs the canonical rules payload consumed by both single-path fab_get_rules and batch planContext. get_rules remains the authoritative single-path API with revision/stale envelope plus audit logging; plan_context is the planning-only batch wrapper that dedupes paths and repeated rule files per entry. Main drift: fab_plan_context MCP output schema still omits description_stubs although resolver/docs expect parity.

description: Explore stable-id metadata compilation path: HTML comment carrier, sync-meta extraction, agents.meta schema changes, revision computation, and doctor/lint integration.
findings: Using the accepted baseline and current codebase structure, sync-meta is the natural compilation seam for stable identities. The stable-id chain should be: HTML comment declaration in rule files, sync-meta extraction into agents.meta.json, identity_source enum (declared|derived), revision recomputation including identity metadata, and doctor/lint enforcement for missing declarations. The key implementation seam is computeAgentsMeta()/computeRevision() plus shared agents meta types/schemas.

description: Explore ledger path convergence and migration flow: unified path resolver, legacy compatibility, doctor --fix migration, event stream/read path impact, tests/docs/i18n fallout.
findings: Ledger path is still hard-coded to root .intent-ledger.jsonl across server, CLI, and docs, despite prior analysis deciding on .fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl plus explicit doctor --fix migration. Core seam is read-ledger.ts; downstream impact includes SSE/event tailing, HTTP event-store persistence, pre-commit staged-file heuristics, audit/doctor, rehydrate-state, and ledger append. There is no implemented migration/fix flow yet, so compatibility reads, explicit move logic, and coordinated test/docs/i18n/protected-token updates must land together.

description: Explore tooling knowledge registration and rollout safeguards: manifest source-of-truth, optional JSDoc extractor, regression coverage, execution ordering, and implementation split to minimize conflicts.
findings: Tooling knowledge should stay decoupled from the first wave of rules/ledger changes. The safe rollout is: first land path and stable-id infrastructure, then add a minimal tooling manifest as source-of-truth, and only later layer optional JSDoc extraction for JS/TS scripts. Regression coverage must be attached to each earlier wave so later tooling work does not hide protocol regressions. Scope separation is straightforward because tooling manifest/docs can live outside the server/CLI hot path.
